title: Normalized water-data schema (PWS, ContaminantResult, Violation taxonomy, Citation, Snapshot, ExplainerUnit)
context: Before any non-pilot ingestion or explainer generation can proceed, the project needs a stable, validated normalized schema that faithfully represents drinking-water measurement and violation data with full provenance. This schema is provisional until the ADR (wqe-arch-001) is recorded and the snapshot-format decision is made. The schema must capture the full chain from raw source to published explainer, including non-detect handling, violation taxonomy, and the self-approval guard on ExplainerUnit.
objective: Define and implement the normalized water-data schema (TypeScript types + JSON Schema validation) covering: WaterSystem (pwsId, name, type, serviceArea, population, primacyAgency); ContaminantResult (pwsId, contaminantId with CAS/EPA code, measuredValue, detectionLimit, unit normalized+original, mcl, mclg, samplePeriod start+end, resultStatus); Violation (pwsId, contaminantId, category health-based/monitoring-reporting/treatment-technique/other, status, period); Citation (publisher, dataset, url, retrievedDate, sourceVintage, sourceLicense, checksum); Snapshot (id, builtAt, sources, pipelineVersion, checksum); and ExplainerUnit (pwsId, lang, snapshotId, sections, contaminantRefs, reviewStatus, reviewedBy, approvedBy, reviewLogRef, lastReviewed, contentLicense). Build-time validation must reject any record missing required Citation fields and any ExplainerUnit where approvedBy equals reviewedBy.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema captures pwsId, contaminant identity (CAS/EPA code), measuredValue + detectionLimit with non-detect preserved as non-detect (not zero), unit (normalized + original recorded), mcl, mclg, samplePeriod (when sampled, not when published), and resultStatus
Violation taxonomy distinguishes health-based vs. monitoring/reporting vs. treatment-technique; resolved vs. open status is preserved

Every ContaminantResult and Violation requires a Citation (publisher/dataset/url/retrievedDate/sourceVintage/license/checksum) tied to a Snapshot; build-time validation rejects a record missing any required Citation field
ExplainerUnit schema enforces reviewedBy != approvedBy; build-time validation rejects a unit where approvedBy equals reviewedBy
Schema format follows the snapshot-format ADR (wqe-arch-001); schema is marked provisional until that ADR lands
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass
output: TypeScript schema definitions and JSON Schema (packages/ or pipeline/schema/) with build-time validation that rejects incomplete citation records and self-approved ExplainerUnit entries; schema documented in the repo.
